name: black_exact_five_priority
notes: Black exact five wins and is not forbidden.

name: black_overline_six
notes: Black creates six contiguous stones without exact-five priority.

name: white_overline_not_black_forbidden
notes: Forbidden detector classifies black candidate moves only; white overline is a white win rule, not black forbidden.

name: simple_cross_double_four
notes: Candidate completes horizontal and vertical four threats through the same point.

name: true_cross_double_three
notes: Candidate is the intersection of two true open-threes.

name: fake_open_three_edge
notes: O_XXX_ near the right edge is not a true open-three because neither gain can make an open four.

name: fake_open_three_blocked_both_sides
notes: O_XXX_O is an apparent three but not a true open-three.

name: revived_three_not_through_candidate
notes: If another line becomes a true three but does not pass through the candidate, it must not count toward double-three for this move.

name: gain_square_overline_makes_three_false
notes: Primary candidate creates overline; exact-five priority does not apply.

name: single_straight_open_three_only
notes: One true straight open-three is not forbidden by itself.

name: single_broken_open_three_only
notes: One true broken open-three is not forbidden by itself.

name: broken_three_cross_double_three
notes: Candidate is the intersection of two broken true open-threes.

name: mixed_straight_and_broken_double_three
notes: Candidate combines one straight and one broken true open-three.

name: single_open_four_only
notes: One four threat is not forbidden by itself.

name: broken_four_cross_double_four
notes: Candidate creates two independent broken-four threats.

name: exact_five_with_vertical_overline_priority
notes: Candidate makes an exact five in one direction, so it is treated as a winning move even though another direction is overline-like.

name: edge_single_open_three_only
notes: A single open-three near the edge is still not forbidden.

name: edge_fake_three_blocked_by_board
notes: The board edge prevents a real open-four continuation.

name: blocked_single_four_not_forbid
notes: A blocked single four is not double-four.

name: diagonal_double_three
notes: Diagonal and anti-diagonal true open-threes count as independent directions.

name: diagonal_double_four
notes: Diagonal and anti-diagonal four threats count as independent directions.

name: overline_diagonal
notes: Diagonal six-in-a-row is a black overline.

name: same_line_df_solid_h
notes: Same-line double-four (horizontal); BBB_X_BBB: two straight fours, gaps adjacent to the candidate. Both completions are exact fives, not overline. Confirmed against Rapfi and renju_forbid.

name: same_line_df_solid_v
notes: Same-line double-four (vertical); BBB_X_BBB: two straight fours, gaps adjacent to the candidate. Both completions are exact fives, not overline. Confirmed against Rapfi and renju_forbid.

name: same_line_df_solid_dd
notes: Same-line double-four (main diagonal); BBB_X_BBB: two straight fours, gaps adjacent to the candidate. Both completions are exact fives, not overline. Confirmed against Rapfi and renju_forbid.

name: same_line_df_solid_du
notes: Same-line double-four (anti-diagonal); BBB_X_BBB: two straight fours, gaps adjacent to the candidate. Both completions are exact fives, not overline. Confirmed against Rapfi and renju_forbid.

name: same_line_df_broken_h
notes: Same-line double-four (horizontal); BB_BX_BB: two broken fours, completion gaps two cells from the candidate. Both completions are exact fives, not overline. Confirmed against Rapfi and renju_forbid.

name: same_line_df_broken_v
notes: Same-line double-four (vertical); BB_BX_BB: two broken fours, completion gaps two cells from the candidate. Both completions are exact fives, not overline. Confirmed against Rapfi and renju_forbid.

name: same_line_df_broken_dd
notes: Same-line double-four (main diagonal); BB_BX_BB: two broken fours, completion gaps two cells from the candidate. Both completions are exact fives, not overline. Confirmed against Rapfi and renju_forbid.

name: same_line_df_split_h

name: recursive_fake_three_both_gains_forbidden
notes: Recursive fake open-three: candidate makes a vertical true open-three and a visual horizontal open-three (6,7,8). Both horizontal extension squares (5,7) and (9,7) are themselves double-four forbidden (each carries a vertical four trap), so neither can legally make an open four. The horizontal line is therefore not a true open-three and the candidate is only a single true three => none. Exercises the is_legal_black_gain recursion. Confirmed against Rapfi and renju_forbid.

name: recursive_control_only_right_gain_forbidden
notes: Control for the recursive case: only the right extension (9,7) is trapped as double-four; the left extension (5,7) is still legal, so the horizontal line is a true open-three and combines with the vertical true three => double_three. Shows the recursion is load-bearing. Confirmed against Rapfi and renju_forbid.

name: recursive_control_only_left_gain_forbidden
notes: Control for the recursive case: only the left extension (5,7) is trapped; the right extension (9,7) is still legal => double_three. Confirmed against Rapfi and renju_forbid.

name: overline_and_double_three_coexist
notes: Both forbidden conditions hold: the candidate makes a horizontal seven-in-a-row (overline) AND a genuine vertical+diagonal double-three (verified by removing the horizontal stones). The point is forbidden either way; all three tools agree on that. The detector reports a single primary reason with priority exact_five > overline > double_four > double_three, hence overline. renju_forbid reports double_three for the same point; that is a reporting-convention difference, not a legality disagreement (see oracle_mismatches.jsonl).
